content.title: A Chronicle of Timekeeping

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: According to archaeological evidence, at least 5,000 years ago, and long before the advent of the Roman Empire, the Babylonians began to measure time, introducing calendars to co-ordinate communal activities, to plan the shipment of goods and, in particular, to regulate planting and harvesting. They based their calendars on three natural cycles: the solar day, marked by the successive periods of light and darkness as the earth rotates on its axis; the lunar month, following the phases of the moon as it orbits the earth; and the solar year, defined by the changing seasons that accompany our planet's revolution around the sun.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Before the invention of artificial light, the moon had greater social impact. And, for those living near the equator in particular, its waxing and waning was more conspicuous than the passing of the seasons. Hence, the calendars that were developed at the lower latitudes were influenced more by the lunar cycle than by the solar year. In more northern climes, however, where seasonal agriculture was practised, the solar year became more crucial. As the Roman Empire expanded northward, it organised its activity chart for the most part around the solar year.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Centuries before the Roman Empire, the Egyptians had formulated a municipal calendar having 12 months of 30 days, with five days added to approximate the solar year. Each period of ten days was marked by the appearance of special groups of stars called decans. At the rise of the star Sirius just before sunrise, which occurred around the all-important annual flooding of the Nile, 12 decans could be seen spanning the heavens. The cosmic significance the Egyptians placed in the 12 decans led them to develop a system in which each interval of darkness (and later, each interval of daylight) was divided into a dozen equal parts. These periods became known as temporal hours because their duration varied according to the changing length of days and nights with the passing of the seasons. Summer hours were long, winter ones short; only at the spring and autumn equinoxes were the hours of daylight and darkness equal. Temporal hours, which were first adopted by the Greeks and then the Romans, who disseminated them through Europe, remained in use for more than 2,500 years.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: In order to track temporal hours during the day, inventors created sundials, which indicate time by the length or direction of the sun's shadow. The sundial's counterpart, the water clock, was designed to measure temporal hours at night. One of the first water clocks was a basin with a small hole near the bottom through which the water dripped out. The falling water level denoted the passing hour as it dipped below hour lines inscribed on the inner surface. Although these devices performed satisfactorily around the Mediterranean, they could not always be depended on in the cloudy and often freezing weather of northern Europe.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The advent of the mechanical clock meant that although it could be adjusted to maintain temporal hours, it was naturally suited to keeping equal ones. With these, however, arose the question of when to begin counting, and so, in the early 14th century, a number of systems evolved. The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts varied according to the start of the count: Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight. Eventually these were superseded by 'small clock', or French, hours, which split the day into two 12-hour periods commencing at midnight.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The earliest recorded weight-driven mechanical clock was built in 1283 in Bedfordshire in England. The revolutionary aspect of this new timekeeper was neither the descending weight that provided its motive force nor the gear wheels (which had been around for at least 1,300 years) that transferred the power; it was the part called the escapement. In the early 1400s came the invention of the coiled spring or fusee which maintained constant force to the gear wheels of the timekeeper despite the changing tension of its mainspring. By the 16th century, a pendulum clock had been devised, but the pendulum swung in a large arc and thus was not very efficient.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: To address this, a variation on the original escapement was invented in 1670, in England. It was called the anchor escapement, which was a lever-based device shaped like a ship's anchor. The motion of a pendulum rocks this device so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel, in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount. Unlike the original form used in early pendulum clocks, the anchor escapement permitted the pendulum to travel in a very small arc. Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Today, highly accurate timekeeping instruments set the beat for most electronic devices. Nearly all computers contain a quartz-crystal clock to regulate their operation. Moreover, not only do time signals beamed down from Global Positioning System satellites calibrate the functions of precision navigation equipment, they do so as well for mobile phones, instant stock-trading systems and nationwide power-distribution grids. So integral have these time-based technologies become to day-to-day existence that our dependency on them is recognised only when they fail to work.

questions.title: a description of an early timekeeping invention affected by cold temperatures
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落D。题干中的“early timekeeping invention”（早期计时发明）在段落D中对应的是“water clock”（水钟），而“affected by cold temperatures”（受寒冷温度影响）对应的是“could not always be depended on in the cloudy and often freezing weather”（在多云且经常寒冷的天气中并不总是可靠的）。因此，段落D最符合题干信息。

central_sentences.sentence: Although these devices performed satisfactorily around the Mediterranean, they could not always be depended on in the cloudy and often freezing weather of northern Europe.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管这些装置在地中海地区表现良好，但在北欧多云且经常寒冷的天气中，它们并不总是可靠的。

target_sen.target_sen: The sundial's counterpart, the water clock, was designed to measure temporal hours at night.
One of the first water clocks was a basin with a small hole near the bottom through which the water dripped out.

syn_ary.synonym: could not always be depended on in the cloudy and often freezing weather

target_sen.target_sen: Although these devices performed satisfactorily around the Mediterranean, they could not always be depended on in the cloudy and often freezing weather of northern Europe.

questions.title: an explanation of the importance of geography in the development of the calendar in farming communities
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落B，因为段落B详细解释了地理位置（赤道附近和北方气候）对日历发展的影响，特别是在农业社区中的作用。段落B提到在赤道附近，月亮周期对日历的影响更大，而在北方气候中，太阳年对日历的影响更大，这正是题干中所描述的地理位置对农业社区日历发展的重要性。

central_sentences.sentence: Hence, the calendars that were developed at the lower latitudes were influenced more by the lunar cycle than by the solar year. In more northern climes, however, where seasonal agriculture was practised, the solar year became more crucial.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，在较低纬度地区发展的日历更多地受到月球周期的影响，而不是太阳年。然而，在更北方的气候中，那里实行季节性农业，太阳年变得更加重要。

target_sen.target_sen: And, for those living near the equator in particular, its waxing and waning was more conspicuous than the passing of the seasons.
In more northern climes, however, where seasonal agriculture was practised, the solar year became more crucial.
Hence, the calendars that were developed at the lower latitudes were influenced more by the lunar cycle than by the solar year.
In more northern climes, however, where seasonal agriculture was practised, the solar year became more crucial.

questions.title: a description of the origins of the pendulum clock
questions.analysis: 题干中的“origins of the pendulum clock”可以通过“pendulum clock had been devised”在段落F中找到对应关系。段落F明确提到了摆钟的发明（devised），这与题干中的“origins”相对应。因此，段落F是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: By the 16th century, a pendulum clock had been devised, but the pendulum swung in a large arc and thus was not very efficient.
central_sentences.translation: 到16世纪，摆钟已经被发明出来，但是钟摆摆动的弧度很大，因此效率不高。

target_sen.target_sen: By the 16th century, a pendulum clock had been devised, but the pendulum swung in a large arc and thus was not very efficient.
By the 16th century, a pendulum clock had been devised, but the pendulum swung in a large arc and thus was not very efficient.

questions.title: details of the simultaneous efforts of different societies to calculate time using uniform hours
questions.analysis: 段落E详细描述了不同社会（意大利、巴比伦、天文和德国）如何将一天分为24个相等的部分，并且这些系统最终被法国的小钟时间取代。这正是题干中提到的不同社会同时努力使用相同小时计算时间的详细描述。因此，段落E是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts varied according to the start of the count: Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight.
central_sentences.translation: 将一天分为24个相等部分的方案根据计数起点的不同而有所变化：意大利时间从日落开始计算，巴比伦时间从日出开始，天文时间从正午开始，而在德国一些大型公共钟使用的"大钟"时间则从午夜开始。

target_sen.target_sen: With these, however, arose the question of when to begin counting, and so, in the early 14th century, a number of systems evolved.

The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts varied according to the start of the count: Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight.

The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts varied according to the start of the count: Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight.

The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts varied according to the start of the count: Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight.

The schemes that divided the day into 24 equal parts varied according to the start of the count: Italian hours began at sunset, Babylonian hours at sunrise, astronomical hours at midday and 'great clock' hours, used for some large public clocks in Germany, at midnight.
Eventually these were superseded by 'small clock', or French, hours, which split the day into two 12-hour periods commencing at midnight.

groups.title: List of Nationalities
groups.desc:
Questions 5-8

Look at the following events (Questions 5-8) and the list of nationalities below.
Match each event with the correct nationality, A-E.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

questions.title: They devised a civil calendar in which the months were equal in length.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“devised a civil calendar”在原文中对应“formulated a municipal calendar”，“months were equal in length”对应“months of 30 days”。通过这些同义表达，可以确认题干所述是指埃及人的发明。因此，正确答案是B. Egyptians。

central_sentences.sentence: Centuries before the Roman Empire, the Egyptians had formulated a municipal calendar having 12 months of 30 days, with five days added to approximate the solar year.
central_sentences.translation: 在罗马帝国出现几个世纪之前，埃及人就制定了一个市政日历，包含12个月，每月30天，并在年末增加5天以接近太阳年。

target_sen.target_sen: Centuries before the Roman Empire, the Egyptians had formulated a municipal calendar having 12 months of 30 days, with five days added to approximate the solar year.
Centuries before the Roman Empire, the Egyptians had formulated a municipal calendar having 12 months of 30 days, with five days added to approximate the solar year.

questions.title: They divided the day into two equal halves.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“divided the day into two equal halves”在原文中对应的表达是“split the day into two 12-hour periods”。原文明确指出这是法国时间系统的特点，因此正确答案是F. French。

central_sentences.sentence: Eventually these were superseded by 'small clock', or French, hours, which split the day into two 12-hour periods commencing at midnight.
central_sentences.translation: 最终，这些系统被"小时钟"或法国时制取代，它将一天分为两个12小时的时段，从午夜开始计时。

target_sen.target_sen: Eventually these were superseded by 'small clock', or French, hours, which split the day into two 12-hour periods commencing at midnight.
Eventually these were superseded by 'small clock', or French, hours, which split the day into two 12-hour periods commencing at midnight.

questions.title: They developed a new cabinet shape for a type of timekeeper.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“new cabinet shape for a type of timekeeper”在原文中对应的是“new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock”。这段描述明确指出了这种新柜形设计是由英国人发明的，因此答案是D. English。

central_sentences.sentence: Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，这项发明允许使用一个长摆，可以每秒摆动一次，从而促进了新的落地式柜设计的发展，后来被称为落地大钟（祖父钟）。

target_sen.target_sen: Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.
Unlike the original form used in early pendulum clocks, the anchor escapement permitted the pendulum to travel in a very small arc.

Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.

questions.title: They created a calendar to organise public events and work schedules.
questions.analysis: 题干问的是谁创建了用来组织公共活动和工作时间的日历。根据文章内容，巴比伦人至少在5000年前就开始测量时间，并推出了日历来协调社区活动、计划货物运输和调节种植和收割。因此，正确答案是 A: Babylonians。

central_sentences.sentence: According to archaeological evidence, at least 5, 000 years ago, and long before the advent of the Roman Empire, the Babylonians began to measure time, introducing calendars to co-ordinate communal activities, to plan the shipment of goods and, in particular, to regulate planting and harvesting. They
central_sentences.translation: 根据考古证据，至少在5000年前，早在罗马帝国出现之前，巴比伦人就开始测量时间，引入日历来协调公共活动，计划货物运输，特别是用来调节种植和收获。

target_sen.target_sen: According to archaeological evidence, at least 5, 000 years ago, and long before the advent of the Roman Empire, the Babylonians began to measure time, introducing calendars to co-ordinate communal activities, to plan the shipment of goods and, in particular, to regulate planting and harvesting.

They based their calendars on three natural cycles: the solar day, marked by the successive periods of light and darkness as the earth rotates on its axis; the lunar month, following the phases of the moon as it orbits the earth; and the solar year, defined by the changing seasons that accompany our planet's revolution around the sun.

groups.title: How the 1670 lever-based device worked
groups.desc:
Questions 9-13

Label the diagram below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 9-13.

questions.answer: ship's anchor / anchor / an anchor / the anchor
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "escapement (resembling 9 ________________)" 提示我们需要找到一种擒纵机构，并且它的形状像某种物体。文章中提到 "anchor escapement" 是一种 "shaped like a ship's anchor" 的装置，这与题干中的 "resembling" 对应。因此，正确答案是 "ship's anchor" 或 "anchor"。学生需要注意的是，"lever-based device" 和 "variation" 虽然也是对擒纵机构的描述，但它们并没有回答形状的问题，所以不正确。

central_sentences.sentence: It was called the anchor escapement, which was a lever-based device shaped like a ship's anchor.
central_sentences.translation: 它被称为锚式擒纵机构，是一种形状像船锚的杠杆装置。

target_sen.target_sen: To address this, a variation on the original escapement was invented in 1670, in England.
It was called the anchor escapement, which was a lever-based device shaped like a ship's anchor.
Unlike the original form used in early pendulum clocks, the anchor escapement permitted the pendulum to travel in a very small arc.
It was called the anchor escapement, which was a lever-based device shaped like a ship's anchor.

questions.analysis: 图示中的 "the 10" 部分是一个齿轮。文章中提到 "escape wheel" 是这个齿轮的名称。通过上下文可以看出，"escape wheel" 是钟表中的一个重要部件，负责精确控制齿轮的转动。因此，"escape wheel" 或 "wheel" 是正确答案。学生需要注意的是，虽然 "gear" 和 "tooth" 也与齿轮相关，但它们并不是文章中描述的具体部件名称。

central_sentences.sentence: The motion of a pendulum rocks this device so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel, in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount.
central_sentences.translation: 钟摆的运动使这个装置摇摆，从而捕捉并释放逃逸轮的每个齿，进而使其精确地转动。

target_sen.target_sen: The motion of a pendulum rocks this device so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel, in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount.
The motion of a pendulum rocks this device so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel, in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount.

questions.analysis: 图中第11题所指的部分是齿轮的一个具体部分。通过观察图示，可以看出这是齿轮的一个小部分，通常称为“齿”。在文章中，描述了擒纵机构的工作原理，其中提到“it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel”，这里的“tooth”指的就是齿轮的齿。虽然学生可能会错填“gear”或“wheel”，但这些词指的是整个齿轮，而不是具体的齿。因此，正确答案是“tooth”。

central_sentences.sentence: The motion of a pendulum rocks this device so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel, in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount.
central_sentences.translation: 钟摆的运动使这个装置摇摆，从而捕捉并释放擒纵轮的每一个齿，依次使其转动一个精确的量。

target_sen.target_sen: The motion of a pendulum rocks this device so that it catches and then releases each tooth of the escape wheel, in turn allowing it to turn a precise amount.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "beats" 和 "each" 在原文中对应 "beat" 和 "once"。通过找到这两个关键词所在的句子，可以确定答案句为 "Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock."。在这句话中，"long pendulum" 是直接描述钟摆的词，因此答案是 "long pendulum" 或 "pendulum"。

central_sentences.sentence: Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，这项发明允许使用一个长钟摆，它可以每秒摆动一次，从而导致了一种新的落地钟设计的发展，这种设计后来被称为祖父钟。

target_sen.target_sen: Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.

Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.

questions.analysis: 题干中提到的“beats each”在原文中对应的是“beat once”，而“beat once a second”明确指出了钟摆每秒摆动一次。因此，答案是“second”。学生需要注意的是，题干中的“each”与原文中的“once”是同义替换，而“second”是时间单位，表示每秒。

central_sentences.sentence: Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，这项发明允许使用一个可以每秒摆动一次的长钟摆，从而导致了一种新的落地钟设计的发展，这种设计后来被称为祖父钟。

target_sen.target_sen: Moreover, this invention allowed the use of a long pendulum which could beat once a second and thus led to the development of a new floor-standing case design, which became known as the grandfather clock.

content.title: AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL IN THE USA
content.summary:
{'A': '1956年在大峡谷上空发生的一起事故导致了联邦航空管理局（FAA）的建立，以监管和监督美国上空飞行器的运营，因为美国上空的飞行器数量日益拥挤。由此产生的空中交通管制结构极大地提高了美国的飞行安全，类似的空中交通管制程序也在世界其他地区实施。', 'B': '20世纪20年代早期，最早的空中交通管制人员通过灯光和旗帜手动引导飞机在机场附近飞行，而在跨国航线上则设置了信标和闪光灯来建立最早的航空线路，然而这种纯视觉系统在恶劣天气下无效，到了20世纪30年代，无线电通信开始用于空中交通管制，纽约市成为第一个拥有类似今天空中交通管制的地区，其他主要都市区紧随其后。', 'C': '在二战期间，ATC中心利用新开发的雷达和改进的无线电通信，但系统仍然很简陋。直到FAA的创建，美国的空域才得到全面的监管，而喷气发动机的出现使得飞机速度大大增加，减少了飞行员的错误容忍度，迫切需要一套规则来确保飞行安全。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了空中交通管制（ATC）不仅仅是在机场前面的雷达屏幕前指挥进出航班的控制员，而是需要适应不同种类、不同目的、不同天气条件的飞机的空域管理结构。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了为了应对挑战，美国实施了一系列措施，其中包括将ATC扩展到几乎整个美国，并且通过控制空域的划分，使得飞行员可以选择在受限制的空域内飞行以获得FAA的保护，或者选择在无限制的空域内飞行以避免受到FAA的规定限制。
简短总结：为了满足挑战，美国实施了ATC扩展和控制空域划分，以满足不同飞行需求。', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了FAA对于飞行环境的认可，根据气象条件的好坏，分为可视飞行规则（VFR）和仪表飞行规则（IFR），并且只有持有仪表评级的飞行员才能选择IFR飞行计划。

简短总结：FAA对飞行环境进行了分类，根据气象条件的好坏分为VFR和IFR，只有持有仪表评级的飞行员才能选择IFR飞行计划。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了受控空域和不受控空域的划分以及各种空域的特点和规定。
简短总结：这段话主要介绍了不同类型的空域划分和规定，包括受控空域和不受控空域的划分，以及各种空域对不同类型飞机的使用和规定的要求。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested. The resulting structure of air traffic control has greatly increased the safety of flight in the United States, and similar air traffic control procedures are also in place over much of the rest of the world.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Rudimentary air traffic control (ATC) existed well before the Grand Canyon disaster. As early as the 1920s, the earliest air traffic controllers manually guided aircraft in the vicinity of the airports, using lights and flags, while beacons and flashing lights were placed along cross-country routes to establish the earliest airways. However, this purely visual system was useless in bad weather, and, by the 1930s, radio communication was coming into use for ATC. The first region to have something approximating today's ATC was New York City, with other major metropolitan areas following soon after.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: In the 1940s, ATC centres could and did take advantage of the newly developed radar and improved radio communication brought about by the Second World War, but the system remained rudimentary. It was only after the creation of the FAA that full-scale regulation of America's airspace took place, and this was fortuitous, for the advent of the jet engine suddenly resulted in a large number of very fast planes, reducing pilots' margin of error and practically demanding some set of rules to keep everyone well separated and operating safely in the air.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Many people think that ATC consists of a row of controllers sitting in front of their radar screens at the nation's airports, telling arriving and departing traffic what to do. This is a very incomplete part of the picture. The FAA realised that the airspace over the United States would at any time have many different kinds of planes, flying for many different purposes, in a variety of weather conditions, and the same kind of structure was needed to accommodate all of them.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: To meet this challenge, the following elements were put into effect. First, ATC extends over virtually the entire United States. In general, from 365m above the ground and higher, the entire country is blanketed by controlled airspace. In certain areas, mainly near airports, controlled airspace extends down to 215m above the ground, and, in the immediate vicinity of an airport, all the way down to the surface. Controlled airspace is that airspace in which FAA regulations apply. Elsewhere, in uncontrolled airspace, pilots are bound by fewer regulations. In this way, the recreational pilot who simply wishes to go flying for a while without all the restrictions imposed by the FAA has only to stay in uncontrolled airspace, below 365m, while the pilot who does want the protection afforded by ATC can easily enter the controlled airspace.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The FAA then recognised two types of operating environments. In good meteorological conditions, flying would be permitted under Visual Flight Rules (VFR), which suggests a strong reliance on visual cues to maintain an acceptable level of safety. Poor visibility necessitated a set of Instrument Flight Rules (IFR), under which the pilot relied on altitude and navigational information provided by the plane's instrument panel to fly safely. On a clear day, a pilot in controlled airspace can choose a VFR or IFR flight plan, and the FAA regulations were devised in a way which accommodates both VFR and IFR operations in the same airspace. However, a pilot can only choose to fly IFR if they possess an instrument rating which is above and beyond the basic pilot's license that must also be held.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Controlled airspace is divided into several different types, designated by letters of the alphabet. Uncontrolled airspace is designated Class F, while controlled airspace below 5,490m above sea level and not in the vicinity of an airport is Class E. All airspace above 5,490m is designated Class A. The reason for the division of Class E and Class A airspace stems from the type of planes operating in them. Generally, Class E airspace is where one finds general aviation aircraft (few of which can climb above 5,490m anyway), and commercial turboprop aircraft. Above 5,490m is the realm of the heavy jets, since jet engines operate more efficiently at higher altitudes. The difference between Class E and A airspace is that in Class A, all operations are IFR, and pilots must be instrument-rated, that is, skilled and licensed in aircraft instrumentation. This is because ATC control of the entire space is essential. Three other types of airspace, Classes D, C and B, govern the vicinity of airports. These correspond roughly to small municipal, medium-sized metropolitan and major metropolitan airports respectively, and encompass an increasingly rigorous set of regulations. For example, all a VFR pilot has to do to enter Class C airspace is establish two-way radio contact with ATC. No explicit permission from ATC to enter is needed, although the pilot must continue to obey all regulations governing VFR flight. To enter Class B airspace, such as on approach to a major metropolitan airport, an explicit ATC clearance is required. The private pilot who cruises without permission into this airspace risks losing their license.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 14-19

Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs, A-G. 
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

Example Answer
Paragraph B x - First steps towards ATC

questions.analysis: Section A部分的标题之所以是“ii: Aviation disaster prompts action”，是因为文章明确描述了一个航空事故及其结果（成立FAA），这一点通过“accident”和“resulted in the establishment of the FAA”这样的同义表达非常容易确认。学生在理解文章时，关键是找到事件和后果的对应关系，同时避开与文章内容不符的干扰选项。

central_sentences.sentence: An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested.
central_sentences.translation: 1956年发生在大峡谷上空的一起事故导致了联邦航空管理局（FAA）的成立，以规范和监督日益拥挤的美国领空中的飞机运行。

syn_ary.synonym: An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956

target_sen.target_sen: An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested.

syn_ary.synonym: resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)

target_sen.target_sen: An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested.

questions.analysis: Section C描述了两个重要的发展：二战带来的新技术（雷达和改进的无线电通信）和喷气发动机的出现。这两个事件促使了FAA的建立和全面的空域管制。标题iii：“Two coincidental developments”准确概括了这一段的主要内容，因为它提到了两个同时发生的重要发展。

central_sentences.sentence: It was only after the creation of the FAA that full-scale regulation of America's airspace took place, and this was fortuitous, for the advent of the jet engine suddenly resulted in a large number of very fast planes, reducing pilots' margin of error and practically demanding some set of rules to keep everyone well separated and operating safely in the air.
central_sentences.translation: 只有在FAA（联邦航空管理局）成立之后，美国的空域才开始全面受到管制，而这恰好是及时的，因为喷气发动机的出现突然导致了大量高速飞机的出现，这减少了飞行员的错误余地，实际上要求制定一套规则来保证所有人在空中保持良好的间隔并安全运行。

target_sen.target_sen: It was only after the creation of the FAA that full-scale regulation of America's airspace took place, and this was fortuitous, for the advent of the jet engine suddenly resulted in a large number of very fast planes, reducing pilots' margin of error and practically demanding some set of rules to keep everyone well separated and operating safely in the air.

syn_ary.synonym: newly developed radar and improved radio communication

target_sen.target_sen: In the 1940s, ATC centres could and did take advantage of the newly developed radar and improved radio communication brought about by the Second World War, but the system remained rudimentary.

It was only after the creation of the FAA that full-scale regulation of America's airspace took place, and this was fortuitous, for the advent of the jet engine suddenly resulted in a large number of very fast planes, reducing pilots' margin of error and practically demanding some set of rules to keep everyone well separated and operating safely in the air.

questions.analysis: Section D的内容指出，许多人对ATC的理解过于简单化，认为它只是控制员在雷达屏幕前指挥交通。文章明确表示这种看法是不完整的，用了 "a very incomplete part of the picture" 来表达。这与小标题 "v: An oversimplified view" 的意思完全一致，因此这个小标题是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Many people think that ATC consists of a row of controllers sitting in front of their radar screens at the nation's airports, telling arriving and departing traffic what to do. This is a very incomplete part of the picture.
central_sentences.translation: 许多人认为空中交通管制(ATC)就是一排管制员坐在国家机场的雷达屏幕前，告诉抵达和离开的航班该做什么。这只是一个非常不完整的画面。

syn_ary.synonym: very incomplete part of the picture

target_sen.target_sen: This is a very incomplete part of the picture.

questions.analysis: 这部分内容主要讨论了不同高度区域的管制空域设置，直接对应小标题iv：“Setting altitude zones”。文章详细描述了从365米以上到215米以上的不同高度区域的空域分类和规则，这与“设定高度区域”这一标题完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: First, ATC extends over virtually the entire United States. In general, from 365m above the ground and higher, the entire country is blanketed by controlled airspace. In certain areas, mainly near airports, controlled airspace extends down to 215m above the ground, and, in the immediate vicinity of an airport, all the way down to the surface.
central_sentences.translation: 首先，空中交通管制（ATC）覆盖了几乎整个美国。总的来说，从地面以上365米及更高的空域，整个国家都被管制空域覆盖。在某些区域，主要是靠近机场的地方，管制空域延伸到地面以上215米，而在机场附近，管制空域一直延伸到地面。

target_sen.target_sen: In general, from 365m above the ground and higher, the entire country is blanketed by controlled airspace.
In certain areas, mainly near airports, controlled airspace extends down to 215m above the ground, and, in the immediate vicinity of an airport, all the way down to the surface.

questions.analysis: Section F的内容主要讲述了FAA根据不同的天气条件（良好的气象条件和能见度差）设定了两种飞行规则（VFR和IFR）。这与标题“viii: Setting rules to weather conditions”直接对应，因为标题中的“rules”与“Flight Rules”同义，而“weather conditions”与“meteorological conditions”同义。因此，Section F的最佳标题是“viii: Setting rules to weather conditions”。

central_sentences.sentence: The FAA then recognised two types of operating environments. In good meteorological conditions, flying would be permitted under Visual Flight Rules (VFR), which suggests a strong reliance on visual cues to maintain an acceptable level of safety. Poor visibility necessitated a set of Instrumental Flight Rules (IFR), under which the pilot relied on altitude and navigational information provided by the plane's instrument panel to fly safely.
central_sentences.translation: 联邦航空管理局（FAA）认识到两种操作环境。在良好的气象条件下，允许按照目视飞行规则（VFR）飞行，这意味着高度依赖视觉线索来保持可接受的安全水平。能见度差时则需要一套仪表飞行规则（IFR），在这种情况下，飞行员依靠飞机仪表盘提供的高度和导航信息来安全飞行。

target_sen.target_sen: In good meteorological conditions, flying would be permitted under Visual Flight Rules (VFR), which suggests a strong reliance on visual cues to maintain an acceptable level of safety.
In good meteorological conditions, flying would be permitted under Visual Flight Rules (VFR), which suggests a strong reliance on visual cues to maintain an acceptable level of safety.
Poor visibility necessitated a set of Instrumental Flight Rules (IFR), under which the pilot relied on altitude and navigational information provided by the plane's instrument panel to fly safely.

questions.analysis: Section G详细描述了不同类型的空域及其对应的规定，这与小标题vii: Defining airspace categories（定义空域类别）直接对应。文章中提到的“Controlled airspace is divided into several different types, designated by letters of the alphabet”与小标题中的“Defining airspace categories”意思相同。

central_sentences.sentence: Controlled airspace is divided into several different types, designated by letters of the alphabet.
central_sentences.translation: 受控空域被划分为几种不同的类型，用字母表中的字母来表示。

syn_ary.synonym: Controlled airspace is divided into several different types

target_sen.target_sen: Controlled airspace is divided into several different types, designated by letters of the alphabet.

syn_ary.synonym: designated by letters of the alphabet

target_sen.target_sen: Controlled airspace is divided into several different types, designated by letters of the alphabet.

groups.desc:
Questions 20-26

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 2? 
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The FAA was created as a result of the introduction of the jet engine.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是 "The FAA was created as a result of the introduction of the jet engine."，意思是FAA的成立是因为喷气发动机的引入。而原文明确指出，FAA的成立是因为1956年大峡谷上空发生的事故。因此，题干中的信息与原文内容相反，正确答案是 "FALSE"。

central_sentences.sentence: An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested.
central_sentences.translation: 1956年在大峡谷上空发生的一起事故导致了联邦航空管理局（FAA）的成立，以监管和监督美国上空日益拥挤的飞机运营。

target_sen.target_sen: An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested.

An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested.

An accident that occurred in the skies over the Grand Canyon in 1956 resulted in the establishment of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to regulate and oversee the operation of aircraft in the skies over the United States, which were becoming quite congested.

questions.title: Air Traffic Control started after the Grand Canyon crash in 1956.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“空中交通管制在1956年大峡谷事故之后才开始”，而原文明确指出“初步的空中交通管制早在大峡谷事故之前就已经存在”。这直接否定了题干的说法，因此答案是FALSE。通过识别题干中的关键词“Air Traffic Control”、“started”和“after the Grand Canyon crash in 1956”，并在原文中找到对应的同义替换词，可以确定答案句，从而得出正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Rudimentary air traffic control (ATC) existed well before the Grand Canyon disaster.
central_sentences.translation: 初步的空中交通管制在大峡谷灾难之前就已经存在了。

target_sen.target_sen: Rudimentary air traffic control (ATC) existed well before the Grand Canyon disaster.
Rudimentary air traffic control (ATC) existed well before the Grand Canyon disaster.
Rudimentary air traffic control (ATC) existed well before the Grand Canyon disaster.

questions.title: Beacons and flashing lights are still used by ATC today.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“beacons”和“flashing lights”在原文中确实出现了，但原文只提到这些设备在20世纪20年代被使用，并没有提到它们今天是否仍然使用。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的支持或反对信息，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: As early as the 1920s, the earliest air traffic controllers manually guided aircraft in the vicinity of the airports, using lights and flags, while beacons and flashing lights were placed along cross-country routes to establish the earliest airways.
central_sentences.translation: 早在20世纪20年代，最早的空中交通管制员就在机场附近用灯光和旗帜手动引导飞机，同时在跨国航线上安装信标和闪光灯来建立最早的航线。

target_sen.target_sen: As early as the 1920s, the earliest air traffic controllers manually guided aircraft in the vicinity of the airports, using lights and flags, while beacons and flashing lights were placed along cross-country routes to establish the earliest airways.
Rudimentary air traffic control (ATC) existed well before the Grand Canyon disaster.

As early as the 1920s, the earliest air traffic controllers manually guided aircraft in the vicinity of the airports, using lights and flags, while beacons and flashing lights were placed along cross-country routes to establish the earliest airways.
Rudimentary air traffic control (ATC) existed well before the Grand Canyon disaster.
However, this purely visual system was useless in bad weather, and, by the 1930s, radio communication was coming into use for ATC.
The first region to have something approximating today's ATC was New York City, with other major metropolitan areas following soon after.

questions.title: Some improvements were made in radio communication during World War II.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "improvements" 对应原文中的 "improved"，"radio communication" 在原文中直接出现，"World War II" 对应 "Second World War"。这些同义替换词在原文中找到了直接对应的句子，说明在第二次世界大战期间确实有无线电通信的改进。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: In the 1940s, ATC centres could and did take advantage of the newly developed radar and improved radio communication brought about by the Second World War, but the system remained rudimentary.
central_sentences.translation: 在20世纪40年代，空中交通管制中心能够并确实利用了第二次世界大战带来的新开发的雷达和改进的无线电通信，但系统仍然处于初级阶段。

target_sen.target_sen: In the 1940s, ATC centres could and did take advantage of the newly developed radar and improved radio communication brought about by the Second World War, but the system remained rudimentary.
In the 1940s, ATC centres could and did take advantage of the newly developed radar and improved radio communication brought about by the Second World War, but the system remained rudimentary.

questions.title: Class F airspace is airspace which is below 365m and not near airports.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "Class F airspace is airspace which is below 365m and not near airports" 可以通过文章中的描述推断出来。文章明确指出 "Uncontrolled airspace is designated Class F"，而 "controlled airspace below 5,490m above sea level and not in the vicinity of an airport is Class E"。这意味着在365米以下且不在机场附近的空域是非管制空域，即F类空域。因此，题干中的描述是正确的，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Uncontrolled airspace is designated Class F, while controlled airspace below 5, 490m above sea level and not in the vicinity of an airport is Class E. All
central_sentences.translation: 未受控制的空域被指定为F类，而海平面以上5,490米以下且不在机场附近的受控空域为E类。

target_sen.target_sen: Uncontrolled airspace is designated Class F, while controlled airspace below 5, 490m above sea level and not in the vicinity of an airport is Class E. All airspace above 5, 490m is designated Class A.

Uncontrolled airspace is designated Class F, while controlled airspace below 5, 490m above sea level and not in the vicinity of an airport is Class E. All airspace above 5, 490m is designated Class A.

syn_ary.synonym: not in the vicinity of an airport

target_sen.target_sen: Uncontrolled airspace is designated Class F, while controlled airspace below 5, 490m above sea level and not in the vicinity of an airport is Class E. All airspace above 5, 490m is designated Class A.

questions.title: All aircraft in Class E airspace must use IFR.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "All aircraft in Class E airspace must use IFR" 表示所有在 Class E 空域的飞机都必须使用 IFR。然而，原文明确指出 "The difference between Class E and A airspace is that in Class A, all operations are IFR"，这意味着只有在 Class A 空域中，所有操作才必须使用 IFR，而 Class E 空域并没有这个要求。因此，题干的陈述是错误的，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The difference between Class E and A airspace is that in Class A, all operations are IFR, and pilots must be instrument-rated, that is, skilled and licensed in aircraft instrumentation.
central_sentences.translation: Class E和Class A空域的区别在于，在Class A空域中，所有操作都必须遵循仪表飞行规则（IFR），且飞行员必须具备仪表等级，即在飞机仪表操作方面有技能和执照。

target_sen.target_sen: Uncontrolled airspace is designated Class F, while controlled airspace below 5, 490m above sea level and not in the vicinity of an airport is Class E. All airspace above 5, 490m is designated Class A.
The reason for the division of Class E and Class A airspace stems from the type of planes operating in them.
Generally, Class E airspace is where one finds general aviation aircraft (few of which can climb above 5, 490m anyway), and commercial turboprop aircraft.
The difference between Class E and A airspace is that in Class A, all operations are IFR, and pilots must be instrument-rated, that is, skilled and licensed in aircraft instrumentation.

questions.title: A pilot entering Class C airspace is flying over an average-sized city.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Class C airspace" 和 "average-sized city" 在原文中分别对应 "Class C" 和 "medium-sized metropolitan"。原文明确指出，Class C 空域对应中等规模的都市机场，这与题干中的 "average-sized city" 是同义表达。因此，题干中的信息在原文中找到了对应，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Three other types of airspace, Classes D, C and B, govern the vicinity of airports. These correspond roughly to small municipal, medium-sized metropolitan and major metropolitan airports respectively, and encompass an increasingly rigorous set of regulations.
central_sentences.translation: 另外三种类型的空域，即D类、C类和B类，管理机场周边区域。这些空域大致对应于小型市政、中等规模都市和大型都市机场，并包含一套越来越严格的规定。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, all a VFR pilot has to do to enter Class C airspace is establish two-way radio contact with ATC.
These correspond roughly to small municipal, medium-sized metropolitan and major metropolitan airports respectively, and encompass an increasingly rigorous set of regulations.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Since the 1970s, parapsychologists at leading universities and research institutes around the world have risked the derision of sceptical colleagues by putting the various claims for telepathy to the test in dozens of rigorous scientific studies. The results and their implications are dividing even the researchers who uncovered them.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Some researchers say the results constitute compelling evidence that telepathy is genuine. Other parapsychologists believe the field is on the brink of collapse, having tried to produce definitive scientific proof and failed. Sceptics and advocates alike do concur on one issue, however: that the most impressive evidence so far has come from the so-called 'ganzfeld' experiments, a German term that means 'whole field'. Reports of telepathic experiences had by people during meditation led parapsychologists to suspect that telepathy might involve 'signals' passing between people that were so faint that they were usually swamped by normal brain activity. In this case, such signals might be more easily detected by those experiencing meditation-like tranquillity in a relaxing 'whole field' of light, sound and warmth.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The ganzfeld experiment tries to recreate these conditions with participants sitting in soft reclining chairs in a sealed room, listening to relaxing sounds while their eyes are covered with special filters letting in only soft pink light. In early ganzfeld experiments, the telepathy test involved identification of a picture chosen from a random selection of four taken from a large image bank. The idea was that a person acting as a 'sender' would attempt to beam the image over to the 'receiver' relaxing in the sealed room. Once the session was over, this person was asked to identify which of the four images had been used. Random guessing would give a hit-rate of 25 per cent; if telepathy is real, however, the hit-rate would be higher. In 1982, the results from the first ganzfeld studies were analysed by one of its pioneers, the American parapsychologist Charles Honorton. They pointed to typical hit-rates of better than 30 per cent - a small effect, but one which statistical tests suggested could not be put down to chance.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The implication was that the ganzfeld method had revealed real evidence for telepathy. But there was a crucial flaw in this argument - one routinely overlooked in more conventional areas of science. Just because chance had been ruled out as an explanation did not prove telepathy must exist; there were many other ways of getting positive results. These ranged from 'sensory leakage' - where clues about the pictures accidentally reach the receiver - to outright fraud. In response, the researchers issued a review of all the ganzfeld studies done up to 1985 to show that 80 per cent had found statistically significant evidence. However, they also agreed that there were still too many problems in the experiments which could lead to positive results, and they drew up a list demanding new standards for future research.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: After this, many researchers switched to autoganzfeld tests - an automated variant of the technique which used computers to perform many of the key tasks such as the random selection of images. By minimising human involvement, the idea was to minimise the risk of flawed results. In 1987, results from hundreds of autoganzfeld tests were studied by Honorton in a 'meta-analysis', a statistical technique for finding the overall results from a set of studies. Though less compelling than before, the outcome was still impressive.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Yet some parapsychologists remain disturbed by the lack of consistency between individual ganzfeld studies. Defenders of telepathy point out that demanding impressive evidence from every study ignores one basic statistical fact: it takes large samples to detect small effects. If, as current results suggest, telepathy produces hit-rates only marginally above the 25 per cent expected by chance, it's unlikely to be detected by a typical ganzfeld study involving around 40 people: the group is just not big enough. Only when many studies are combined in a meta-analysis will the faint signal of telepathy really become apparent. And that is what researchers do seem to be finding.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: What they are certainly not finding, however, is any change in attitude of mainstream scientists: most still totally reject the very idea of telepathy. The problem stems at least in part from the lack of any plausible mechanism for telepathy.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Various theories have been put forward, many focusing on esoteric ideas from theoretical physics. They include 'quantum entanglement', in which events affecting one group of atoms instantly affect another group, no matter how far apart they may be. While physicists have demonstrated entanglement with specially prepared atoms, no-one knows if it also exists between atoms making up human minds. Answering such questions would transform parapsychology. This has prompted some researchers to argue that the future lies not in collecting more evidence for telepathy, but in probing possible mechanisms. Some work has begun already, with researchers trying to identify people who are particularly successful in autoganzfeld trials. Early results show that creative and artistic people do much better than average: in one study at the University of Edinburgh, musicians achieved a hit-rate of 56 per cent. Perhaps more tests like these will eventually give the researchers the evidence they are seeking and strengthen the case for the existence of telepathy.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-G, below. 
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: the discovery of a mechanism for telepathy.
the need to create a suitable environment for telepathy.
their claims of a high success rate.
a solution to the problem posed by random guessing.
the significance of the ganzfeld experiments.
a more careful selection of subjects.
a need to keep altering conditions.

questions.title: Researchers with differing attitudes towards telepathy agree on
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 E: the significance of the ganzfeld experiments。文章中明确提到，无论是怀疑论者还是支持者，他们都同意Ganzfeld实验是迄今为止最令人印象深刻的证据。这与题干中的“Researchers with differing attitudes towards telepathy agree on”完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Sceptics and advocates alike do concur on one issue, however: that the most impressive evidence so far has come from the so-called 'ganzfeld' experiments, a German term that means 'whole field'.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，怀疑论者和支持者都一致认同一个问题：迄今为止最令人印象深刻的证据来自于所谓的"甘兹费尔德"实验，这是一个德语词，意为"整体场"。

target_sen.target_sen: Sceptics and advocates alike do concur on one issue, however: that the most impressive evidence so far has come from the so-called 'ganzfeld' experiments, a German term that means 'whole field'.

locating_words.locating_word: Researchers with differing attitudes towards telepathy

target_sen.target_sen: Sceptics and advocates alike do concur on one issue, however: that the most impressive evidence so far has come from the so-called 'ganzfeld' experiments, a German term that means 'whole field'.

questions.title: Reports of experiences during meditation indicated
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Reports of experiences during meditation”在原文中对应的是“Reports of telepathic experiences had by people during meditation”，而“indicated”对应的是“led parapsychologists to suspect”。原文提到，冥想期间的体验报告让科学家们怀疑心灵感应需要一个特定的环境，这样的环境可以帮助检测到微弱的信号。因此，正确答案是B: the need to create a suitable environment for telepathy。

central_sentences.sentence: In this case, such signals might be more easily detected by those experiencing meditation-like tranquillity in a relaxing 'whole field' of light, sound and warmth.
central_sentences.translation: 在这种情况下，这些信号可能更容易被那些在光、声音和温暖的放松"整体场"中体验类似冥想宁静的人所检测到。

syn_ary.synonym: Reports of telepathic experiences had by people during meditation

target_sen.target_sen: Reports of telepathic experiences had by people during meditation led parapsychologists to suspect that telepathy might involve 'signals' passing between people that were so faint that they were usually swamped by normal brain activity.

questions.title: Attitudes to parapsychology would alter drastically with
questions.analysis: 文章明确指出，主流科学家对超心理学（心灵感应）的态度没有改变，主要问题在于缺乏合理的心灵感应机制。因此，题干中的“Attitudes to parapsychology would alter drastically with”最直接对应的答案是“the discovery of a mechanism for telepathy”。

central_sentences.sentence: What they are certainly not finding, however, is any change in attitude of mainstream scientists: most still totally reject the very idea of telepathy. The problem stems at least in part from the lack of any plausible mechanism for telepathy.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，他们确实没有发现主流科学家的态度有任何改变：大多数人仍然完全拒绝心灵感应的想法。这个问题至少部分源于缺乏任何合理的心灵感应机制。

target_sen.target_sen: What they are certainly not finding, however, is any change in attitude of mainstream scientists: most still totally reject the very idea of telepathy.
What they are certainly not finding, however, is any change in attitude of mainstream scientists: most still totally reject the very idea of telepathy.
The problem stems at least in part from the lack of any plausible mechanism for telepathy.
What they are certainly not finding, however, is any change in attitude of mainstream scientists: most still totally reject the very idea of telepathy.

questions.title: Recent autoganzfeld trials suggest that success rates will improve with
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是F: a more careful selection of subjects。文章中提到，研究人员正在尝试识别在autoganzfeld试验中特别成功的人，早期结果显示，具有创造性和艺术性的人表现得比平均水平好得多。这表明，如果选择合适的受试者，成功率会提高。因此，题干中的“success rates will improve with”与文章中的“do much better than average”相对应，答案是F。

central_sentences.sentence: Early results show that creative and artistic people do much better than average: in one study at the University of Edinburgh, musicians achieved a hit-rate of 56 per cent.
central_sentences.translation: 早期结果显示，有创造力和艺术才能的人表现远超平均水平：在爱丁堡大学的一项研究中，音乐家达到了56%的命中率。

target_sen.target_sen: Early results show that creative and artistic people do much better than average: in one study at the University of Edinburgh, musicians achieved a hit-rate of 56 per cent.
Early results show that creative and artistic people do much better than average: in one study at the University of Edinburgh, musicians achieved a hit-rate of 56 per cent.

groups.title: Telepathy Experiments
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the table below. 
Choose NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS from the passage in each gap.

table.title: Telepathy Experiments
content: Involved a person acting as a $1595$, who picked out one$1596$from a random selection of four, and a $1597$, who then tried to identify it.
Hit-rates were higher than with random guessing.
Positive results could be produced by factors such as $1598$$1599$
$1600$were used for key tasks to limit the amount of $1601$in carrying out the tests.
The results were then subjected to a$1602$
The$1603$between different test results was put down to the fact that sample groups were not $1604$(as with most ganzfeld studies).

questions.analysis: 答案是“sender”，因为题干中的“acting as a”在原文中直接对应“acting as a 'sender'”。通过这个对应关系，我们可以确定答案是“sender”。此外，题干中的“who picked out one from a random selection of four”在原文中对应的是“would attempt to beam the image over to the 'receiver'”，这进一步确认了“sender”是正确的答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The idea was that a person acting as a 'sender' would attempt to beam the image over to the 'receiver' relaxing in the sealed room.
central_sentences.translation: 这个实验的想法是，一个充当"发送者"的人会试图将图像传送给在密闭房间里放松的"接收者"。

target_sen.target_sen: The idea was that a person acting as a 'sender' would attempt to beam the image over to the 'receiver' relaxing in the sealed room.

questions.analysis: 答案是“picture”或“image”，因为题干中的“picked out one ... from a random selection of four”在原文中对应的是“beam the image over ... random selection of four”。原文明确提到“image”是从四张图像中随机选择的，因此答案是“picture”或“image”。

central_sentences.sentence: The idea was that a person acting as a 'sender' would attempt to beam the image over to the 'receiver' relaxing in the sealed room. Once the session was over, this person was asked to identify which of the four images had been used.
central_sentences.translation: 这个实验的设想是，一个人作为"发送者"会试图将图像传送给在密闭房间中放松的"接收者"。一旦实验结束，这个人就被要求辨认出四张图像中哪一张被使用了。

syn_ary.synonym: chosen from a random selection of four

target_sen.target_sen: In early ganzfeld experiments, the telepathy test involved identification of a picture chosen from a random selection of four taken from a large image bank.

syn_ary.synonym: asked to identify which of the four images had been used

target_sen.target_sen: Once the session was over, this person was asked to identify which of the four images had been used.

questions.analysis: 答案是“receiver”，因为题干中的“acting as a”在原文中直接对应“acting as a 'sender'”，而题干中的“who then tried to identify it”在原文中对应“this person was asked to identify which of the four images had been used”。根据这些对应关系，可以确定“receiver”是题干中需要填入的内容。

central_sentences.sentence: The idea was that a person acting as a 'sender' would attempt to beam the image over to the 'receiver' relaxing in the sealed room. Once the session was over, this person was asked to identify which of the four images had been used.
central_sentences.translation: 实验的设想是，一个充当"发送者"的人会试图将图像传送给在密闭房间里放松的"接收者"。一旦实验结束，这个人（接收者）被要求识别出四张图像中哪一张被使用了。

target_sen.target_sen: The idea was that a person acting as a 'sender' would attempt to beam the image over to the 'receiver' relaxing in the sealed room.
Once the session was over, this person was asked to identify which of the four images had been used.

questions.analysis: 答案是“sensory leakage/outright fraud/fraud”，因为文章中明确提到“many other ways of getting positive results”包括“sensory leakage”和“outright fraud”。这些因素是导致“positive results”的具体原因，直接对应题干中的“factors”。

central_sentences.sentence: These ranged from 'sensory leakage' - where clues about the pictures accidentally reach the receiver - to outright fraud.
central_sentences.translation: 这些因素从"感官泄露"（即关于图片的线索意外地传达给接收者）到彻底的欺诈。

target_sen.target_sen: Just because chance had been ruled out as an explanation did not prove telepathy must exist; there were many other ways of getting positive results.
However, they also agreed that there were still too many problems in the experiments which could lead to positive results, and they drew up a list demanding new standards for future research.
Just because chance had been ruled out as an explanation did not prove telepathy must exist; there were many other ways of getting positive results.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "sensory leakage" 和 "outright fraud"。这是因为题干中的 "Positive results" 和 "factors such as" 对应原文中的 "positive results" 和 "many other ways"，而这些因素在原文中具体列举为 "sensory leakage" 和 "outright fraud"。因此，这两个词是导致正面结果的因素，符合题干要求。

central_sentences.sentence: These ranged from 'sensory leakage' - where clues about the pictures accidentally reach the receiver - to outright fraud.
central_sentences.translation: 这些因素从"感官泄漏"（即有关图片的线索意外地传达给接收者）到彻底的欺诈。

target_sen.target_sen: Just because chance had been ruled out as an explanation did not prove telepathy must exist; there were many other ways of getting positive results.
However, they also agreed that there were still too many problems in the experiments which could lead to positive results, and they drew up a list demanding new standards for future research.
But there was a crucial flaw in this argument - one routinely overlooked in more conventional areas of science.
Just because chance had been ruled out as an explanation did not prove telepathy must exist; there were many other ways of getting positive results.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "computers"。题干中的 "key tasks" 在原文中直接对应 "key tasks"，而 "limit the amount of" 对应 "minimise"。原文提到 "used computers to perform many of the key tasks" 和 "minimise human involvement"，这表明计算机被用来执行关键任务以减少人为干预，从而减少错误结果的风险。因此，正确答案是 "computers"。

central_sentences.sentence: After this, many researchers switched to autoganzfeld tests - an automated variant of the technique which used computers to perform many of the key tasks such as the random selection of images.
central_sentences.translation: 在这之后，许多研究人员转向了自动钢场实验——这是一种自动化的技术变体，使用计算机来执行许多关键任务，如随机选择图像。

target_sen.target_sen: After this, many researchers switched to autoganzfeld tests - an automated variant of the technique which used computers to perform many of the key tasks such as the random selection of images.
In 1987, results from hundreds of autoganzfeld tests were studied by Honorton in a 'meta-analysis', a statistical technique for finding the overall results from a set of studies.
After this, many researchers switched to autoganzfeld tests - an automated variant of the technique which used computers to perform many of the key tasks such as the random selection of images.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "human involvement"。题干中的 "key tasks" 对应原文中的 "key tasks"，"limit the amount of" 对应原文中的 "minimising"。原文提到 "minimising human involvement" 是为了减少错误，因此可以推测出要填的内容是 "human involvement"。

central_sentences.sentence: After this, many researchers switched to autoganzfeld tests - an automated variant of the technique which used computers to perform many of the key tasks such as the random selection of images. By minimising human involvement, the idea was to minimise the risk of flawed results.
central_sentences.translation: 在这之后，许多研究人员转向了自动化的ganzfeld测试——这是一种使用计算机执行许多关键任务（如随机选择图像）的自动化变体技术。通过最小化人为参与，目的是最小化出现错误结果的风险。

target_sen.target_sen: After this, many researchers switched to autoganzfeld tests - an automated variant of the technique which used computers to perform many of the key tasks such as the random selection of images.
By minimising human involvement, the idea was to minimise the risk of flawed results.

questions.analysis: 答案是“meta-analysis”，因为题干中的“Autoganzfeld studies 1987”对应文章中的“results from hundreds of autoganzfeld tests”，而题干中的“Result”对应文章中的“were studied by Honorton in a 'meta-analysis'”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案是“meta-analysis”。

central_sentences.sentence: In 1987, results from hundreds of autoganzfeld tests were studied by Honorton in a 'meta-analysis', a statistical technique for finding the overall results from a set of studies.
central_sentences.translation: 1987年，Honorton对数百次自动完整视野实验的结果进行了"元分析"，这是一种用于寻找一系列研究整体结果的统计技术。

syn_ary.synonym: In 1987, results from hundreds of autoganzfeld tests

target_sen.target_sen: In 1987, results from hundreds of autoganzfeld tests were studied by Honorton in a 'meta-analysis', a statistical technique for finding the overall results from a set of studies.
In 1987, results from hundreds of autoganzfeld tests were studied by Honorton in a 'meta-analysis', a statistical technique for finding the overall results from a set of studies.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“lack of consistency”，因为题干中的“different test results”对应文章中的“individual ganzfeld studies”，“was put down to the fact that”对应“remain disturbed by”。这些同义替换词帮助我们定位到答案句。答案句明确指出了“lack of consistency”是个别Ganzfeld研究之间的问题，因此这个答案是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Yet some parapsychologists remain disturbed by the lack of consistency between individual ganzfeld studies.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，一些超心理学家仍然对个别甘兹菲尔德研究之间缺乏一致性感到困扰。

target_sen.target_sen: Yet some parapsychologists remain disturbed by the lack of consistency between individual ganzfeld studies.

If, as current results suggest, telepathy produces hit-rates only marginally above the 25 per cent expected by chance, it's unlikely to be detected by a typical ganzfeld study involving around 40 people: the group is just not big enough.

If, as current results suggest, telepathy produces hit-rates only marginally above the 25 per cent expected by chance, it's unlikely to be detected by a typical ganzfeld study involving around 40 people: the group is just not big enough.

questions.analysis: 答案是“big enough”或“large enough”，因为题干中的“sample groups were not”在原文中对应的是“the group is just not big enough”。这表明样本组的规模不够大，导致了不同测试结果之间的差异。通过找到题干中的线索词“sample groups”和“not”，并在原文中找到对应的同义替换词“the group”和“just not”，我们可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: If, as current results suggest, telepathy produces hit-rates only marginally above the 25 per cent expected by chance, it's unlikely to be detected by a typical ganzfeld study involving around 40 people: the group is just not big enough.
central_sentences.translation: 如果按照目前的结果推测，心灵感应产生的命中率仅略高于偶然情况下预期的25%，那么在一个典型的只涉及约40人的完形实验中就不太可能被检测到：这个群体规模就是不够大。

target_sen.target_sen: If, as current results suggest, telepathy produces hit-rates only marginally above the 25 per cent expected by chance, it's unlikely to be detected by a typical ganzfeld study involving around 40 people: the group is just not big enough.

If, as current results suggest, telepathy produces hit-rates only marginally above the 25 per cent expected by chance, it's unlikely to be detected by a typical ganzfeld study involving around 40 people: the group is just not big enough.
